mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 1.15% remains below the inflation rate (CPI at 244.048, implying ~6% YoY), creating negative real rates that incentivize equity allocation over bonds. This persistent monetary accommodation drives down real Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities, reducing demand for safe-haven bonds.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates negative at 1.15% nominal vs 6% inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite negative real rates, or equity allocation does not increase relative to bonds in institutional flows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 146,615k persons with unemployment at 4.3% signals a tight labor market. Sustained labor tightness without significant deterioration over the next 20 trading days maintains wage pressure expectations, which the Fed interprets as requiring higher rates, pushing Treasury yields upward as market prices in future rate hikes.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 10 basis points despite stable employment and low unemployment, or Fed communication explicitly signals pause in rate hiking cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 478,873M and housing starts (HOUST) at 1,215k units indicate resilient consumer demand. If this demand persists without contraction signals over the next 20 trading days, it sustains inflation expectations and forces the Fed to maintain higher rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0% AND HOUST > 1,100k units
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales and housing remain above recessionary thresholds
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 12 basis points despite sustained retail sales and housing starts, or both RSAFS and HOUST decline sequentially over the 20-day period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 105.16 (2017=100) shows supply-side resilience. Over the next 20 trading days, if INDPRO remains flat or grows, it signals non-recessionary supply conditions that allow the Fed to maintain restrictive policy without fear of demand destruction, keeping Treasury yields elevated.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 15 basis points despite stable industrial production, or INDPRO declines more than 1% MoM signaling demand weakness.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 17,010.7B (last quarterly reading) combined with current labor market tightness and retail sales strength suggests the economy remains in expansion. Over a 60-day horizon, if no recession signals emerge, the Fed maintains higher rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in a longer period of restrictive policy.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth trajectory remains positive AND PAYEMS does not decline > 100k
evidence_refs: fact:No recession indicators visible in latest data
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 25 basis points over 60 days despite positive GDP growth signals, or PAYEMS declines by more than 200k persons in any single month.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 244.048 (index level) reflects persistent inflation above the Fed's 2% target. If inflation remains sticky over the next 5 trading days without signs of imminent decline, market participants expect the Fed to signal continued rate maintenance or further hikes, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward immediately.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI index at 244.048 reflecting elevated inflation
regime_hint: above-target inflation regime requiring monetary restraint
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 8 basis points over 5 trading days despite sticky CPI readings, or CPI declines more than 0.5% MoM signaling rapid disinflation.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment at 4.3% combined with payroll employment at 146,615k creates a regime where labor market slack is minimal. If unemployment falls below 4.0% over the next 60 trading days, it signals overheating labor market dynamics that force the Fed to maintain or raise rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market extends its rate-hike expectations.
regime_hint: tight labor market with overheating risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 20 basis points over 60 days despite unemployment falling below 4.0%, or Fed explicitly signals pivot to easing policy.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,215k units and retail sales (RSAFS) at 478,873M both signal strong demand. Over the next 20 trading days, if both series decline sequentially by more than 3%, it signals demand destruction that forces the Fed to pivot toward easing, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as rate-cut expectations increase.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 3% MoM AND RSAFS declines > 3% MoM
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 10 basis points despite both HOUST and RSAFS declining > 3%, or Fed does not signal easing intentions within 20 trading days of demand deterioration.
